notes: Verified via WebSearch 2026-07-19 (Arabic Wikipedia, aps.dz official Algerian news agency, radioalgerie.dz): 19 May 1956 general student strike called by UGEMA (الاتحاد العام للطلبة المسلمين الجزائريين), in support of the Algerian War of Independence.

Strike spanned Algeria plus Algerian students in French, Egyptian, and Tunisian universities; extended to secondary-school students too; many joined the FLN's armed wing (ALN) directly in the mountains.
Annually commemorated 19 May as 'يوم الطالب' — radioalgerie.dz's 2024 article references the '68th anniversary', confirming consistent annual recurrence since 1956.

1956 appears as a bare year throughout content but is well below the hardcoded-year threshold (current year - 1 = 2025), so no validator issue — it's a genuine historical fact citation, not a stale 'this year' reference.
Explicitly stated this is a commemorative national day, NOT an official paid public holiday in Algeria's calendar — no source found classifying it as such, stated clearly to avoid misleading readers.

Deliberately safe content scope: internal Algerian student mobilization history, NOT the flagged-sensitive French-colonial-violence topics (Sétif/Guelma massacre, Paris massacre) that need explicit owner sign-off per the Algeria research pass.
Single-country event (_countryCode: dz), aliasSlugs: [] to avoid base_country_leakage.
